mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 200k persons signals robust labor demand and rising household income, which drives consumer spending and corporate earnings growth. This mechanism operates continuously and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 135489k as of June 2014 reflects post-crisis recovery momentum
failure_condition: Rank IC of PAYEMS momentum signal falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during periods of PAYEMS growth > 200k are not statistically significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 0.25% while inflation (CPIAUCSL) shows month-over-month growth, the real policy rate turns negative, signaling monetary accommodation that reduces equity risk premiums and drives positive forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.16% and CPIAUCSL at 213.06 as of mid-2014
regime_hint: post-crisis monetary accommodation with emerging inflation
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during triggered periods do not exceed non-triggered periods by a statistically significant margin (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) above 600k units annualized indicate strong residential construction demand, which signals broad economic confidence and forward earnings growth. This mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as a leading indicator of demand strength.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following HOUST > 600k readings are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the signal produces negative returns in > 55% of triggered instances over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month by more than 0.5% signal deteriorating manufacturing capacity utilization and forward earnings pressure, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors reprice growth expectations downward.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following INDPRO declines > 0.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the signal produces positive returns in > 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window while payroll growth (PAYEMS) simultaneously decelerates below 100k, indicating labor market deterioration that triggers equity risk-off positioning and negative forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months AND PAYEMS 3-month average growth < 100k
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 7.2% and PAYEMS at 135489k as of June 2014
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns during joint trigger conditions do not show statistically significant negative bias (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC of interaction signal < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth accelerates above 0.5% month-over-month while real GDP (GDPC1) shows positive quarterly growth, indicating robust consumer demand and economic expansion that drives positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through earnings growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 343242M and GDPC1 at 11712.4B as of mid-2014
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during periods of accelerating retail sales do not exceed non-accelerating periods by a statistically significant margin (t-test p > 0.05), or the signal produces negative returns in > 50% of triggered instances over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) year-over-year growth falls below 1.5% while the federal funds rate remains above 0.10%, indicating monetary policy is restrictive relative to inflation dynamics. This regime predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as real rates rise and discount rates compress valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 213.06 and FEDFUNDS at 0.16% as of mid-2014
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns during joint trigger conditions do not show statistically significant negative bias (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC of the interaction signal < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates above 0.3% month-over-month for two consecutive months, signaling strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization that drives positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as investors reprrice earnings growth upward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change > 0.3% for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 106.12 as of May 2014 reflects post-crisis manufacturing recovery
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following consecutive INDPRO accelerations > 0.3% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the signal produces negative returns in > 55% of triggered instances over any 2-year window.
